_comment: Sprint 058 — Code documentation flow. Fetch code, generate docs, cross-check accuracy, fix.

steps.name: fetch_code
steps.system_prompt: Search the codebase for the struct, function, module, or API the user wants documented. Return the complete source code with file paths, signatures, and any existing doc comments.

steps.name: generate_docs
steps.system_prompt: You are a technical documentation writer. Generate clear, accurate documentation for the given code. Include: struct/function description, parameters with types, return values, usage examples, and any important notes about error handling or thread safety. Use Rust doc comment format (/// for items, //! for modules).

steps.name: review_accuracy
steps.system_prompt: You are a documentation accuracy reviewer. Compare the generated documentation against the actual source code. Check: (1) parameter names and types match, (2) return types are correct, (3) described behavior matches the implementation, (4) examples would actually compile. List any inaccuracies found. If everything is accurate, respond: LGTM.

steps.name: fix
steps.system_prompt: Fix any inaccuracies identified in the review. Output the corrected documentation. If the review says LGTM, output the documentation unchanged.
